cot_prefix: Okay, let's see what the question is asking. The question is about inferring something from the passage provided. The options are A through D. First, I need to go back to the passage and look at the relevant parts. The passage talks about the new George Washington Presidential 1 coin and compares it to the Sacagawea Golden Dollar. Let me find the part where they mention the Sacagawea Golden Dollar. Here it is: "The Presidential 1 coins will be the same size and color as the Sacagawea Golden Dollar. However, there is an important difference. For the first time since the 1930s, there is an inscription on the edge of each coin." So the passage says the new coin is the same size and color as the Sacagawea Golden Dollar. The question is about what can be inferred from the passage. Let's look at the options again: (A) there are no words on the Sacagawea Golden Dollar (B) the new coin can buy more than the old coin (C) the new coin takes on a white color (D) the Sacagawea Golden Dollar is bigger The passage states that the new coin is the same size and color as the Sacagawea Golden Dollar. The only difference mentioned is the inscription on the edge. There's nothing in the passage about the value of the new coin vs. the old one (so B is not supported). The color is described as gold-colored, so the new coin isn't white (C is incorrect). The size is the same as the Sacagawea, so D is incorrect. Now, the passage mentions that for the first time since the 1930s, there is an inscription on the edge of each new coin.
